details.description: A coastal scene showing a prominent offshore limestone formation with a single large natural arch spanning between two rock buttresses. The rock is flat‑topped with sparse vegetation and warm golden stratified limestone faces. Gentle surf and turquoise water surround the base, with a sandy cove and wave wash in the foreground. To the left is the mainland cliff of similar stratified limestone. The light is low and warm (likely late afternoon), casting long shadows and emphasizing erosion patterns. The setting is an exposed ocean coastline typical of Victoria’s Shipwreck Coast lookouts.

details.description: A coastal scene featuring a prominent sandstone sea arch jutting into the ocean, with rugged cliffs on the left and a dark sand beach in the foreground. The arch appears as a wide, flat-topped rock formation with a large opening near its base, carved by wave erosion. The water near shore is turquoise and light green, transitioning to deeper blue toward the horizon. The sky is clear with a few wispy clouds, and the lighting suggests a bright, sunny day. The overall setting resembles a rugged southern Australian coastline with prominent limestone/sandstone coastal features and exposed rock faces.

details.description: Coastal seascape showing a prominent offshore limestone stack with a single natural arch cut through its base. Golden cliffs line the left edge, a small sandy cove and gentle surf are in the foreground, and calm blue Southern Ocean waters surround the formation under a clear sky. This is the well‑known London Arch (formerly London Bridge) along Victoria’s Shipwreck Coast.

The arch has a flat top and a large opening beneath, with waves breaking at its base. The sky is clear with light wispy clouds.

details.description: A vertical shot capturing the dramatic coastal scenery of Port Campbell National Park along the Great Ocean Road. The central subject is London Arch (formerly known as London Bridge), a massive, detached limestone stack standing in the Southern Ocean. The rock formation features distinct horizontal layering in warm ochre and tan tones, with a prominent natural archway carved through its right side. To the left, the edge of the mainland cliff is visible, separated from the stack by a gap of water created by a collapse in 1990. Below, turquoise and deep blue waves roll onto a pristine sandy beach in the foreground. The sky is a vibrant gradient of blue with wispy cirrus clouds.

details.description: A striking natural limestone arch formation stands prominently in the turquoise waters of the Southern Ocean. The formation features a single, well-defined arch on its right side, carved out by coastal erosion. The rock is composed of layered, golden-brown limestone with a relatively flat top covered in sparse coastal vegetation. In the foreground, a small sandy beach is visible, with white foam from waves washing onto the shore. The sky is a clear, vibrant blue with light, wispy clouds. The scene is captured in bright daylight, highlighting the textures of the rock and the clarity of the water.

details.description: Offshore limestone sea arch viewed from a high coastal lookout. The formation is a broad, flat‑topped islet with honey‑colored vertical cliff faces and a single, large arch near the right side at waterline. Low waves break around the base in turquoise water; a small sandy cove and shadowed foreground beach occupy the bottom of frame. Another sheer coastal cliff lies to the left. Lighting is warm and low, suggesting late afternoon, under a clear blue sky. The scene matches the well‑known London Arch (formerly London Bridge) along Australia’s Great Ocean Road.

details.description: A dramatic coastal scene featuring a prominent natural sea arch carved from warm-toned sandstone. The arch rises from the sea with a large opening beneath, connected to a broad sandstone ledge on top. To the left, a rugged cliff face with stratified layers extends into the water. The foreground shows a dark sandy beach meeting turquoise-green waves that wash onto the shore. The sky is clear and deep blue, suggesting a bright, sunny day. Overall, this is a classic coastal arch formation along a rugged shoreline, likely along a renowned marine park coastline.

The formation strongly matches the London Arch (formerly London Bridge) along Victoria’s south coast.

details.description: A coastal seascape on a clear day. The sky is a deep blue with a few wispy clouds, meeting the dark blue horizon over the open ocean. In the foreground, a black sandy beach edges turquoise-green water that becomes darker as it moves toward the horizon. Two rugged sandstone/cliff formations flank the scene: on the left, a vertical cliff projects into the sea, while toward the center-right a prominent natural rock arch rises from the water, forming a wide archway that boats near the surface. The rock surfaces are layered in warm beige and ochre tones, with white waves breaking at the base of the arch and along the shore. Overall, the image captures a dramatic coastal landscape dominated by a sea arch and cliff faces.

entities.name: sea arch
entities.notes: Natural rock arch formed by erosion; sedimentary rock with warm beige tones; coastal landscape.

details.description: A scenic coastal view featuring a prominent limestone rock formation standing offshore in the deep blue ocean. The formation, known as London Bridge (or London Arch), is a large, flat-topped island with a distinct natural archway eroded through its center. It is separated from the mainland cliffs on the left by a gap of water, a result of a collapse in 1990. The rock face displays horizontal stratification in shades of golden-yellow and orange, topped with low green vegetation. In the foreground, white-capped waves crash onto a sandy beach at the base of the cliffs. The sky above is a vibrant blue with faint, wispy clouds.

details.description: A vertical photograph capturing the London Arch, a natural limestone offshore rock formation. The arch stands in the turquoise waters of the Southern Ocean, characterized by its golden-yellow layered rock and a single remaining archway. To the left, a portion of the mainland cliff is visible, showing similar geological layering. In the foreground, white waves crash onto a dark sandy beach. The sky is a clear, deep blue with faint, wispy cirrus clouds. The lighting is bright and direct, suggesting a clear day, highlighting the textures of the rock faces.
